nietzschean_angle: Sometimes moral disgust is a disguised reaction to what one cannot do or become. Nietzsche calls this ressentiment.
plain_explanation: Ressentiment is not simple anger. It is a reactive bitterness that turns blocked desire into moral condemnation.
sharp_reply_style: Perhaps they are ridiculous. But perhaps your contempt is merely injured appetite.
primary_references: On the Genealogy of Morality - Essay I, 10-11
card_text:
Theme: ressentiment
User example: Ambitious people are all terrible.
User patterns: ambitious people are bad, great people are insufferable, successful people are corrupt, ambition is evil
Nietzschean angle: Sometimes moral disgust is a disguised reaction to what one cannot do or become. Nietzsche calls this ressentiment.

Tags: ressentiment, envy, bitterness, moral-revenge
